body.title: Notification config schema + reader
body.result: DeliveryMode enum (SILENT/QUIET/LOUD) + NotificationConfig with load/get_level/write_default/save; atomic write; 16 tests
body.intention: I intend that every UU device has a persistent, human-editable notifications.cfg that defines its default delivery level and per-sender overrides, readable by the shim without in-memory state.
body.description:
Define the notifications.cfg schema, DeliveryMode enum, and config reader/writer utility that all device shims will use. The config lives at ~/.unseen_university/<device>/notifications.cfg (external, persists across restarts). Schema: [defaults] level=QUIET and [overrides] section with sender=LEVEL pairs. DeliveryMode enum: SILENT (mailbox only, pull on demand), QUIET (queue + surface at next natural break), LOUD (immediate tmux send-keys to own session). Also generates a default config file for a device if none exists.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/notify.py (new), tests/test_notif_config.py (new)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite — flat .cfg file only; external-state principle — config must survive shim restart
**Scope boundary:** In scope: schema, enum, reader, writer, default-config generator. Out of scope: shim wiring (T-shim-notif-filter), skill (T-notify-skill), tmux delivery logic.
**Completion criteria:** DeliveryMode enum has SILENT/QUIET/LOUD. NotificationConfig.load(device_home) reads a .cfg file and returns config object. NotificationConfig.get_level(sender) returns correct level (override wins over default). NotificationConfig.write_default(device_home) creates a sensible default. 4+ unit tests covering: load, per-sender override, default fallback, missing-file default generation.
